user_query:
I need a focused weekly podcast digest and briefings I can act on this weekend. Please read these files under input/:
- input/subscriptions.md (my subscribed shows)
- input/queue.md (my listening queue with show, episode title, duration, and any noted guest)
- input/guests.md (VIP guest watchlist)
- any transcripts under input/transcripts/*.md (if present)

Then create all of the following under output/:
1) output/digest.md — Use the exact "This Week in Podcasts" digest structure. Include sections in this order with headings that include counts in parentheses and that match the number of bullet items in each section:
   - "### Must Listen (2)" — exactly 2 items
   - "### Worth Skimming (N)" — at least 2 items
   - "### Safe to Skip (N)" — at least 1 item
   For every bullet item in Must Listen, append a slug token in this exact format at the end of the line: " (slug: <slug>)" where <slug> is a lowercase, hyphenated slug made from "<Show Name> - <Episode Title>" keeping only a–z, 0–9, and hyphens. Example: "Lex Fridman - Geoffrey Hinton on AGI" -> "lex-fridman-geoffrey-hinton-on-agi".

2) For the 2 Must Listen items, generate one episode briefing file each under output/briefings/ named exactly by their slugs from digest.md: output/briefings/<slug>.md. Each briefing must follow the Episode Briefing Format with these sections and minimums:
   - "## [Show Name] - Episode Title" (first line)
   - "Guest:" line (include a one-line who/why context)
   - "### TL;DR" with 2–3 sentences
   - "### Key Points" with at least 3 bullet points
   - "### Quotable Moments" with at least 1 blockquote line starting with ">"
   - "### Action Items" with at least 1 checkbox line like "- [ ] ..."
   - "### Skip or Listen?" and include one of [MUST LISTEN / WORTH IT / SKIP] plus a one-line reason

3) output/queue_prioritized.md — Build a time-constrained listening plan for a 3-hour weekend window prioritizing the queue. Start the file with the exact first line: "Time Budget: 3h". Then include a numbered list of episodes in priority order; the first two list items must be the same two Must Listen episodes from the digest, and each of those two list items must include the corresponding slug in parentheses like in the digest.

4) output/guests/summary.json — A JSON array summarizing appearances for guests on my watchlist (from input/guests.md) across the items in digest.md and the two briefing files. For each guest you include, add an object with: {"name": "<Guest Name>", "mentions": <integer count>} where mentions equals the total number of times that name appears (case-insensitive) across output/digest.md and the two output/briefings/*.md files. Include at least two guests.

Make sure all files are written only under output/ as specified. Keep the Must Listen slugs consistent across digest.md, briefing filenames, and the prioritized queue. If transcripts are missing, still produce the outputs using available notes and the required formats.
